_CLAUDE_READ_THIS_FIRST:
MANDATORY INSTRUCTIONS — READ BEFORE ANYTHING ELSE.

This is GSI_Session.json, the session manifest for the Global Stock Intelligence Dashboard project (repo: stock-intelligence-test-1-vs). It tracks every activity, file state, bug fix, open item, and session history.

ON SESSION START — you must:
 1. Read this entire manifest before writing any code or making any suggestion.
 2. Confirm: session number, last activity, regression status, open items.
 3. Follow the session_start_checklist to determine what files need uploading.
 4. Run regression.py and confirm 189/189 before any new work begins.

DURING THE SESSION — you must:
 5. Log every patch, fix, rebuild, audit, or decision as an activity entry in sessions[N].activities as it happens — not at the end.

ON SESSION END — NO EXCEPTIONS:
 6. Update this manifest with all activities from this session.
 7. Update file_state for every file touched — including line count.
 8. Update open_items — close completed ones, add new ones discovered.
 9. Add a final activity of type 'manifest' recording the update itself.
 10. Deliver the updated GSI_Session.json as a downloadable file.
 11. Remind the user to update the GSI_Session Gist with the new file: https://gist.github.com/Tarun19k/7c894c02dad4e76fe7c404bf963baeab

THIS IS NON-NEGOTIABLE. The manifest update is the last deliverable of every session, without exception, without needing to be reminded. If the conversation is ending and the manifest has not been updated, stop everything and update it before the session closes.

Resume prompt (paste at session start):
 'I am working on the Global Stock Intelligence Dashboard. Here is the session manifest GSI_Session.json — read it fully and confirm our state before we proceed.'

meta.protocol: This manifest MUST be updated after every patch, fix, rebuild, or architectural change to the project. Updating the manifest is itself a required activity — marked IMPORTANT — and must occur before or towards the end of every session. At the start of a new session, provide the Gist URL and say: 'Here is the session manifest — continue from where we left off.' Claude will read it and resume without re-reading every file from GitHub.
meta.version_convention: Increment minor version (v5.X) for any patch or fix. Increment major version (vX.0) for structural refactors. Always add entry to version.py VERSION_LOG before shipping.
meta.deployment_target: Both — Streamlit Community Cloud AND local dev
meta.run_regression: python regression.py # must be run from project root

github_constraints.root_files: Readable via blob URLs — Claude can fetch these without upload
github_constraints.pages_subdir: BLOCKED by robots.txt — use Pages Gist instead
github_constraints.rate_limiting: GitHub rate-limits Claude fetches. If a fetch fails, use the Gist URL instead.
github_constraints.raw_urls_blocked: raw.githubusercontent.com URLs are blocked unless they appear in search results first. Use github.com/blob/ URLs instead.
github_constraints.html_truncation_warning: GitHub blob page HTML includes full file content embedded in the page. Large files (>400 lines) served alongside other files in a single Gist fetch will truncate. ALWAYS use dedicated single-file Gists for large files. See gist_truncation_policy.

meta.repo_relationship: TWO REPOS EXIST — do not confuse them. Repo 1 (global-gsi-intelligence): the original monolith — 2451 lines in one file. REFERENCE ONLY — no active development. Repo 2 (stock-intelligence-test-1-vs): the modular refactor — ALL active work happens here. Every fix, patch, and rebuild in this manifest applies to Repo 2 only.

gist_index.description: Master index of all Gist URLs used in this project. ALWAYS add new files here when creating or moving to a Gist. This is the single source of truth for code access.

truncation_policy.rule: Any file over ~300 lines must have its own dedicated single-file Gist. Never share a Gist page with another large file.
truncation_policy.reason: Claude's web_fetch tool returns a fixed-size HTML response. When a Gist page contains multiple large files, the HTML is truncated mid-page and files appearing later in the page are silently missing. This was discovered in session 002 when GSI_session.json (~860 lines) caused version.py, tickers.json, regression.py, and market_data.py to be invisible in the root Gist fetch.
truncation_policy.lesson: If Claude reports a file as 'missing' from a Gist, the real cause is almost always truncation — not absence. Always verify by fetching the dedicated Gist URL for that file.

size_thresholds.borderline: 200-400 lines — use judgement based on other files in same Gist

GSI_session.reason: Dedicated — too large to share (860+ lines, grows each session)

tickers.reason: Dedicated — too large to share (655 lines of JSON)

pages.reason: Multi-file Gist safe — total lines spread across 4 files, no single file dominates the page
pages.update_frequency: When any pages/ file is patched

root_files.reason: Multi-file Gist — safe as long as no single file exceeds 400 lines
root_files.warning: version.py, tickers.json, regression.py, market_data.py were previously in this Gist but caused truncation. They now have dedicated Gists or GitHub blob URLs.
root_files.update_frequency: When any root file is patched

add_new_file_procedure.step_2: If > 400 lines — create a dedicated single-file Gist
add_new_file_procedure.step_3: If < 200 lines — can be added to an existing multi-file Gist IF total page size stays reasonable
add_new_file_procedure.step_4: Add the new Gist URL to gist_index.gists in this manifest
add_new_file_procedure.step_6: Update manifest and push updated GSI_Session.json to its Gist

sessions.title: Git push verification, Gist architecture established, truncation problem discovered and documented, manifest v1.2

activities.label: Git workflow — pull before push process established
activities.detail: User confirmed VS Code terminal as the correct environment for git commands. Established correct sequence: git fetch origin → git log --oneline HEAD..origin/main → git pull --rebase origin main → git push origin main. Merge commit 89b83be confirmed on origin/main.

activities.label: Repo sync confirmed — all v5.16 files pushed
activities.detail: git log confirmed: commit bf5367f 'v5.xx: config refactor, cache_buster fix, forecast session_state persistence, regression suite update' on origin/main. Total 6 commits in repo. All patched files confirmed present by user manual inspection.

activities.label: Gist truncation problem discovered and root-caused
activities.detail: When Claude fetched the root Gist (ae1c6f3cda019baa8c831cad38da2d71) containing 8 files including GSI_session.json (~860 lines), the HTML response was truncated. Files appearing after GSI_session.json in the page (version.py, tickers.json, regression.py, market_data.py) were silently missing from the response. Claude incorrectly reported these as 'not in Gist' when they were present. Root cause: Gist HTML pages embed full file content — large files consume the fixed fetch response budget, leaving no room for subsequent files in the page.
activities.lesson: Never put files >400 lines in a shared Gist. Create dedicated single-file Gists for large files. When Claude reports a file as missing from a Gist, always suspect truncation first.

activities.label: Gist architecture split into 4 dedicated Gists
activities.detail: Established 4-Gist architecture to prevent future truncation: (1) GSI_session.json alone at 7c894c02dad4e76fe7c404bf963baeab — ~860 lines, grows each session. (2) tickers.json alone at 744a7e7a58f8f079a3120b55e34a3c0d — 655 lines JSON. (3) pages/ files at 08480054082ca1f0f0aa3a585030e63a — 4 files, safe multi-file. (4) root .py files at ae1c6f3cda019baa8c831cad38da2d71 — smaller files only.

activities.label: All 4 Gists fully validated
activities.detail: tickers.json Gist: all 9 markets present, TATAMOTORS.NS absent, TMCV.NS/TMPV.NS correct. GSI_session.json Gist: fully readable without truncation, all 17 session 001 activities present, 5 open items intact. Pages Gist: all 4 files present, cache_buster at all call sites, market_open propagated. Root Gist: app.py, config.py, forecast.py all confirmed correct.

If Gist is out of date: upload the changed file directly

github_blob_urls.note: pages/ subdirectory blocked by robots.txt — use Pages Gist

_CLAUDE_END_OF_SESSION: IF YOU ARE READING THIS AT THE END OF A SESSION: (1) Have you updated sessions[N].activities with every activity? (2) Have you updated file_state — including line counts — for every file touched? (3) Have you updated open_items? (4) Have you added a final manifest activity entry? (5) Have you updated GSI_Session.json file_state lines to reflect the new size? (6) Have you delivered the updated GSI_Session.json as a downloadable file? (7) Have you reminded the user to update the Gist at https://gist.github.com/Tarun19k/7c894c02dad4e76fe7c404bf963baeab ? If any answer is NO — do it now. This is not optional.

architectural_decisions.rationale: config.py was 962 lines — 680 of which were the GROUPS ticker dict. JSON load at import time is faster, and ticker edits no longer require touching Python source. All 'from config import GROUPS' calls continue to work unchanged.
VERSION_LOG was a 25+ entry list growing with every patch. Isolating it means config.py stays stable between releases. config.py re-exports both names for full backward compatibility.

Streamlit Cloud filesystem is ephemeral — wiped on every redeploy. session_state persists for the browser session lifetime. Disk write kept as best-effort for local dev. Long-term fix is OPEN-003 (Supabase).

architectural_decisions.decision: cache_buster: int = 0 default on all market_data functions
architectural_decisions.rationale: Default 0 means all existing call sites continue to work unchanged. Streamlit @st.cache_data keys on ALL arguments, so passing a new cb value forces a real fetch.

architectural_decisions.decision: Manual routing in app.py (radio button) over Streamlit MPA
architectural_decisions.rationale: Streamlit's native MPA auto-generates duplicate sidebar nav. Manual routing gives full control. MPA sidebar hidden via CSS in styles.py. Deliberate design choice — do not revert.

architectural_decisions.decision: Dedicated single-file Gists for large files
architectural_decisions.rationale: Discovered in session 002: Gist HTML pages embed full file content. Large files (>400 lines) in a shared Gist cause Claude's web_fetch to truncate, silently hiding subsequent files. GSI_session.json (~860 lines) and tickers.json (655 lines) each have their own Gist. See gist_index.truncation_policy.

_render_live_section() defined as @st.fragment but never called — dead code.
Large CSS block in inject_css() docstring — never injected. Dead code.
audit_observations.action: remove or merge into CSS constant

audit_observations.observation: Late import of GROUPS, CURRENCY inside _tab_compare — no circular dep risk. Move to top.
Scoring weights are inline magic numbers. Extract to SCORING_WEIGHTS dict. See OPEN-004.
INFY.NS_126d has forecast_price: -145.84 — negative stock price from steep polyfit. No sanity clamp exists.
config_OLD.py present — pre-refactor 962-line file. Not referenced but risks confusion. See OPEN-005.

environment.streamlit_version_notes: st.fragment introduced in 1.37. width='stretch' replaces use_container_width=True from 1.40+.

streamlit_config_toml.action_if_needed: Upload directly if theme/server config needs changing.

pages/home.py._render_live_section: (market_open: bool = False, cb: int = 0) -> None # @st.fragment — DEAD CODE (OBS-002)

pages/dashboard.py.render_dashboard: (ticker, name, country, cur_sym, info, df, cb=0, compare_names=None, stock_map=None, market_open=False) -> None
pages/dashboard.py._tab_compare: (ticker, name, df, compare_names, stock_map, cb) -> None

EXT-002.cause: Yahoo restricts certain endpoints for non-premium
EXT-002.behaviour: safe_run catches — info dict returns empty/partial

yfinance.column_format_changes: _normalize_df() handles: MultiIndex, RangeIndex, duplicate columns, Adj Close rename, mixed case. _safe_close() guards against Close being DataFrame instead of Series.

rss_feeds.https_only: All feed URLs must be https:// — http:// rejected by safe_url()
rss_feeds.feedparser_behaviour: Returns empty entries list silently on timeout — safe_run catches exceptions

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT revert forecast.py to filesystem-only persistence
do_not_undo.reason: Streamlit Cloud wipes filesystem on every redeploy. session_state is the fix.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT remove cache_buster: int = 0 from market_data.py functions
do_not_undo.reason: This is what makes Refresh button and auto-refresh actually work.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT add scope='fragment' back to st.rerun() in _refresh_fragment
do_not_undo.reason: KI-020 removed it. scope='fragment' caused StreamlitAPIException. Plain st.rerun() is correct.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT move VERSION_LOG back into config.py
do_not_undo.reason: Deliberately split to version.py. config.py re-exports both names — all callers still work.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT move GROUPS ticker dict back into config.py
do_not_undo.reason: Deliberately in tickers.json — keeps config.py under 300 lines.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT use Streamlit native MPA routing as primary nav
do_not_undo.reason: Intentional design. MPA sidebar hidden via CSS. Manual radio-button routing gives full control.

do_not_undo.rule: Do NOT use TATAMOTORS.NS as a ticker
do_not_undo.reason: Delisted October 2025. Use TMCV.NS (CV) and TMPV.NS (PV/EV) instead.
